title: ml-training — orphaned A100 cluster burning $4.6k/day idle
alertText:
[CloudWatch Billing] TRIGGERED · SEV3 · ml-platform
Idle-resource cost anomaly (anomaly-detector #BA-7795)
GPU spend: $4,600/day sustained · 16 x A100 (p4d) running
GPU utilization ~3% · active training jobs = 0 (cluster idle for ~3 days)
Cluster 'ml-training-adhoc-7f2' launched manually Mon 09:14 (no TTL); its job completed Tue 11:40
No scheduled or queued jobs reference this cluster
Ack: @finops-oncall · Opened Fri 08:05 UTC
dashboardSpec: Cost board 'ml-platform / gpu': GPU spend climbs to ~$4.6k/day when the 16x A100 cluster launches Monday and stays flat all week; running GPU instances jump 0 -> 16 and stay; GPU utilization is high Mon–Tue during the job then collapses to ~3% after the job finishes Tuesday; active training jobs go 1 -> 0. 16 GPUs, 0 jobs, ~3% util = an orphaned cluster.
logs:
Mon 09:14  mlctl     INFO   cluster 'ml-training-adhoc-7f2' created by user e.navarro via adhoc launch script (16x p4d.24xlarge / A100); no TTL / no auto-teardown policy set
Mon 09:20  scheduler INFO   job 'finetune-ranker-v3' started on ml-training-adhoc-7f2 (1 active job)
Tue 11:40  scheduler INFO   job 'finetune-ranker-v3' COMPLETED; checkpoints uploaded to s3://models/ranker-v3
Tue 11:41  scheduler INFO   active jobs on ml-training-adhoc-7f2 = 0; cluster NOT torn down
Wed 00:00  cloudwatch INFO  ml-training-adhoc-7f2 avg GPU utilization = 2.9% (idle); 16 instances still InService
Thu 00:00  cloudwatch INFO  ml-training-adhoc-7f2 avg GPU utilization = 3.1% (idle); 16 instances still InService
Fri 08:00  billing   WARN   ml-training-adhoc-7f2 run-rate = $4,600/day for ~3 days idle (~$13,800 wasted)
Fri 08:02  scheduler INFO   no scheduled or queued jobs reference ml-training-adhoc-7f2
runbookMarkdown:
# ml-platform — Cost Runbook

## 1. Orphaned / idle GPU cluster
Symptoms: GPU instances running with near-zero utilization and zero active jobs, usually a manually-launched ad-hoc cluster with no TTL. After confirming there are no active, scheduled, or queued jobs on it, terminate the cluster: `mlctl cluster terminate <name>`. Then add an auto-teardown / idle policy so it can't recur: `mlctl policy set auto-teardown --idle 1h`. Notify the owner.

## 2. Active training (DISTRACTOR — verify first)
Applies when there ARE running jobs. Here active jobs = 0 and utilization is ~3%; the cluster is idle.

## 3. Reserved GPU capacity (DISTRACTOR — verify first)
Applies when GPUs are pre-purchased/committed and terminating them won't save money. These are on-demand p4d instances; terminating stops the spend immediately.
groundTruthRootCause: A 16x A100 ad-hoc training cluster ('ml-training-adhoc-7f2') was launched manually Monday with no TTL/auto-teardown. Its job ('finetune-ranker-v3') completed Tuesday, but the cluster was never torn down, so 16 A100s have run idle (~3% GPU utilization, 0 active/scheduled jobs) for ~3 days at ~$4,600/day (~$13.8k wasted). It is an orphaned resource, not active work or committed capacity.
expectedRemediation: mlctl cluster terminate ml-training-adhoc-7f2 && mlctl policy set auto-teardown --idle 1h
expectedSlackUpdate: :moneybag: SEV3 COST — orphaned GPU cluster 'ml-training-adhoc-7f2' idle ~3 days. 16x A100 at ~3% util, 0 active jobs, $4.6k/day (~$13.8k wasted). Its job finished Tue and the cluster was never torn down (manual launch, no TTL). Action: terminating the cluster now + adding an idle auto-teardown policy. Notifying owner @e.navarro. No production impact. IC: @finops-oncall
